title: Cerasus pusilliflora
intro: Cerasus pusilliflora, also known as Dwarf Cherry, is a small, deciduous tree or shrub belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to East Asia. The health benefits of Cerasus pusilliflora include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, thanks to its rich content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The bioactive constituents of this plant are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties, which include flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, and phenolic acids like ferulic acid and sinapic acid. Cerasus pusilliflora is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Cerasus pusilliflora is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, Rosaceae. It is a type of cherry or plum tree native to East Asia, specifically in China and Japan. As a member of the genus Cerasus, it is closely related to other species of cherries and plums. However, the taxonomy of Cerasus is still being refined and may be subject to change. The classification of Cerasus pusilliflora is currently as a species within the genus Cerasus in the family Rosaceae.
